page: Microsoft Azure FX24ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmsv2 family, virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 12 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 504.0 GB of system memory, providing 21.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2128.0 and a top-tier text processing score of 2965.0. Multi-core performance is solid, as shown by a Geekbench multi-core score of 15360.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 26678.35. The server demonstrates strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression at 34.45 GB/sec and static web serving throughput at 46.47 GB/sec. For LLM inference using a small 135M model, it achieves a top-tier text generation speed of 420.68 tokens/sec. However, Passmark memory benchmarks place its memory score in the lower tier at 1789.98, indicating potential latency tradeoffs despite the high capacity.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX24ms_v2 offers a high density of memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The inclusion of advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions) enhances its suitability for vector and matrix operations. This server is optimized for large-scale databases, memory-intensive enterprise applications, software compilation, and moderate-scale machine learning inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure FX24ms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the FXmsv2 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 12 physical cores and a 260 MB L3 cache. The server is equipped with 504.0 GB of RAM, translating to 21.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPUs. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core execution, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2128.0 and top-tier text processing capabilities. It also excels in small-model LLM text generation, reaching 420.68 tokens/second. While memory capacity is exceptionally high, Passmark memory benchmarks indicate a lower-tier score, representing a potential latency tradeoff. This server is highly suited for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large databases, and vector-based workloads utilizing its AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX24ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs at 4.2 GHz, 504 GB of RAM, and advanced AMX instruction sets for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX24ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz clock speed.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual server featuring dedicated high-frequency Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
504.0 GB of system memory providing 21.0 GB of RAM per physical core
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix operations
Strong single-core performance and top-tier text processing capabilities
No local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external premium storage
Best suited for memory-intensive databases and enterprise applications
